mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and boosts duration-sensitive valuations, so a 20-day rise in S&P 500 returns follows sustained easing. The signal is confirmed when FEDFUNDS is declining across consecutive monthly observations and short-rate pressure is absent.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the sign reverses in more than 60% of easing observations over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month pace, real yields and policy tightening expectations rise, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation accelerations are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS identifies a deteriorating labor market that reduces earnings expectations and cyclically pressures equities lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when both series move against growth at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if each series alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in RSAFS after a prior high level signals weakening consumer demand and retail revenue growth, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next week as earnings downgrades begin. The signal is intended to capture near-term demand shock rather than trend inflation.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month RSAFS declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return is nonnegative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in INDPRO and HOUST signals improving real activity and goods-sector capacity utilization, supporting cyclicals and broader earnings expectations. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over roughly one quarter when production and housing strengthen together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value over single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A low but rising unemployment rate after a downturn marks a late-stage recessionary labor slump that depresses consumption and margin expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal activates when unemployment is both elevated and still worsening.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rises above the threshold but 20-day SPX returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations, or if the average return is not materially below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth both slow, aggregate demand is weakening across income and spending channels, so equity returns should be negative over the following quarter. The mechanism fails if only one series slows while the other remains strong.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous slowdowns in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when one proxy is removed.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, high or rising FEDFUNDS combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL creates real-rate pressure and valuation compression, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-specific and should not be used during easing.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns during tightening periods, or if the sign weakens to zero outside tightening regimes.
